mission.title: Install and Configure Trivy Operator (Aqua Security)
mission.description: Trivy Operator is a Kubernetes-native security toolkit that helps you identify vulnerabilities in your container images and Kubernetes resources. It is particularly useful for organizations looking to enhance their security posture by automating vulnerability scanning and compliance checks. Key trade-offs include the need for additional resources and potential performance impacts during scans.

steps.title: Add the Aqua Security Helm repository
steps.description:
This step adds the Aqua Security Helm repository to your Helm client, allowing you to install the Trivy Operator.

```bash

helm repo add aqua https://aquasecurity.github.io/helm-charts

helm repo update

```

> This command adds the Aqua Security Helm chart repository.
> Updates the local Helm chart repository cache.

steps.title: Install the Trivy Operator
steps.description:
This step installs the Trivy Operator in the 'trivy-system' namespace using Helm.

```bash

kubectl create namespace trivy-system

helm install trivy-operator aqua/trivy-operator --namespace trivy-system --version 0.23.0

```

> Creates a dedicated namespace for the Trivy Operator.
> Installs the Trivy Operator with the specified version.

steps.title: Verify the installation
steps.description:
This step checks if the Trivy Operator pods are running correctly.

```bash

kubectl get pods -n trivy-system

```

> Lists all pods in the 'trivy-system' namespace to verify their status.

steps.title: Create a TrivyConfiguration resource
steps.description:
This step creates a TrivyConfiguration resource to define how the operator should scan images.

```bash

cat <<EOF | kubectl apply -f -
apiVersion: aquasecurity.github.io/v1alpha1
kind: TrivyConfiguration
metadata:
  name: default
  namespace: trivy-system
spec:
  image: 'aquasec/trivy:0.23.0'
  severity: 'HIGH'
EOF

```

> Creates a default configuration for the Trivy Operator.
commands:
cat <<EOF | kubectl apply -f -
apiVersion: aquasecurity.github.io/v1alpha1
kind: TrivyConfiguration
metadata:
  name: default
  namespace: trivy-system
spec:
  image: 'aquasec/trivy:0.23.0'
  severity: 'HIGH'
EOF

uninstall.title: Uninstall the Trivy Operator
uninstall.description:
This step removes the Trivy Operator and its associated resources. Be aware that this will delete all related configurations and data.

```bash

helm uninstall trivy-operator --namespace trivy-system

kubectl delete namespace trivy-system

```

> Uninstalls the Trivy Operator from the cluster.
> Deletes the namespace and all resources within it.

upgrade.title: Upgrade the Trivy Operator
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you back up any important configurations. This step upgrades the Trivy Operator to the latest version.

```bash

kubectl get pods -n trivy-system -o jsonpath='{.items[*].metadata.name}' | xargs -n 1 kubectl drain --ignore-daemonsets --delete-local-data

helm upgrade trivy-operator aqua/trivy-operator --namespace trivy-system --version 0.23.0

kubectl uncordon $(kubectl get nodes -o name)

```

> Drains the pods to prepare for the upgrade.
> Upgrades the Trivy Operator to the specified version.
> Uncordons the nodes after the upgrade.
commands: kubectl get pods -n trivy-system -o jsonpath='{.items[*].metadata.name}' | xargs -n 1 kubectl drain --ignore-daemonsets --delete-local-data

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'TrivyConfiguration not found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The TrivyConfiguration resource may not have been created or is in a different namespace.

**Fix:**
Ensure the TrivyConfiguration resource exists in the 'trivy-system' namespace using 'kubectl get TrivyConfiguration -n trivy-system'.

troubleshooting.title: Pods in 'trivy-system' namespace are CrashLoopBackOff
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This may be due to misconfiguration or insufficient resources allocated to the operator.

**Fix:**
Check the pod logs using 'kubectl logs <pod-name> -n trivy-system' to identify the issue and adjust resources if necessary.

troubleshooting.title: Vulnerability scanning fails with 'no image found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The Trivy Operator may not have access to the images or the images may not be present.

**Fix:**
Verify that the images exist and that the operator has the necessary permissions to access them.

troubleshooting.title: Helm install fails with 'chart not found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The Aqua Security Helm repository may not be added or updated correctly.

**Fix:**
Re-run 'helm repo add aqua https://aquasecurity.github.io/helm-charts' and 'helm repo update'.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for custom image repositories and enhanced scanning capabilities.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: No specific migration steps required for this version.

resolution.summary: You should see the Trivy Operator pods running in the 'trivy-system' namespace with a status of 'Running'. Use 'kubectl get pods -n trivy-system' to verify.

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running version 1.25 or higher and the necessary tools installed.
